questions.q: What is the application of sodium nitrate in everyday life?
questions.explanation: Sodium nitrate is used as a fertilizer in agriculture, it increases the yield of crops.

questions.q: What is the primary use of silver in everyday life?
opts: (A) As a disinfectant in medical field
(B) As a catalyst in petroleum industry
questions.explanation: Silver is used as a mirror coating because of its good reflective properties.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a non-stick coating?
questions.explanation: Teflon is a non-stick coating, it prevents food from sticking to the surface.

questions.q: What is the main application of titanium in everyday life?
opts: (B) In the manufacture of surgical implants
questions.explanation: Titanium is used in the manufacture of surgical implants due to its biocompatibility.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a waterproofing agent?
questions.explanation: Silicones are used as waterproofing agents, they prevent water from penetrating the surface.

questions.q: What is the primary use of silica in everyday life?
questions.explanation: Silica is used in the manufacture of abrasives, it provides a high degree of hardness.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a ceramic?
questions.explanation: Porcelain is an example of a ceramic, it is made from clay and other minerals.

questions.q: What is the primary use of refractories in everyday life?
questions.explanation: Refractories are used to line furnaces and kilns, they can withstand high temperatures.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a nanomaterial?
questions.explanation: Graphene is an example of a nanomaterial, it has a thickness of only one atom.

questions.q: What is the main application of nanomaterials in everyday life?
questions.explanation: Nanomaterials are used in the manufacture of electronics, they can improve the performance of devices.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a smart material?
questions.explanation: Shape memory alloy is an example of a smart material, it can change its shape in response to temperature.

questions.q: What is the primary use of shape memory alloys in everyday life?
opts: (A) In the manufacture of surgical implants
(C) In the manufacture of medical devices
(D) In the manufacture of automotive parts
questions.explanation: Shape memory alloys are used in the manufacture of surgical implants, they can be used to repair damaged tissues.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a piezoelectric material?
questions.explanation: Lithium niobate is an example of a piezoelectric material, it can generate an electric current in response to mechanical stress.

questions.q: What is the primary use of piezoelectric materials in everyday life?
opts: (A) In the manufacture of medical devices
questions.explanation: Piezoelectric materials are used in the manufacture of transducers, they can convert mechanical energy into electrical energy.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a conductive polymer?
questions.explanation: Polyacetylene is an example of a conductive polymer, it can conduct electricity.

questions.q: What is the primary use of conductive polymers in everyday life?
opts: (A) In the manufacture of medical devices
(D) In the manufacture of conductive inks
questions.explanation: Conductive polymers are used in the manufacture of conductive inks, they can be used to create electronic circuits.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a biodegradable plastic?
questions.explanation: Polylactic acid is an example of a biodegradable plastic, it can break down naturally in the environment.

questions.q: What is the primary use of biodegradable plastics in everyday life?
opts: (A) In the manufacture of packaging materials
(B) In the production of disposable cutlery
(C) In the manufacture of medical devices
questions.explanation: Biodegradable plastics are used in the manufacture of packaging materials, they can reduce the amount of plastic waste.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a self-healing material?
questions.explanation: Hydrogel is an example of a self-healing material, it can repair itself after damage.

questions.q: What is the primary use of self-healing materials in everyday life?
opts: (A) In the manufacture of medical devices
questions.explanation: Self-healing materials are used in the manufacture of coatings, they can protect surfaces from damage.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a metamaterial?
questions.explanation: A metamaterial with negative refractive index is an example of a metamaterial, it has properties not found in nature.
